# @respeak/lucide-motion-vue

> Lucide icons animated with Motion for Vue. 535 tree-shakable Vue 3 SFCs
> with ergonomic triggers (hover/tap/view/programmatic), a composable
> `<AnimateIcon>` wrapper, and a Nuxt module for auto-imports. Peer deps:
> `vue ^3.3`, `motion-v ^2`.

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @respeak/lucide-motion-vue motion-v
# or: npm install / yarn add / bun add
```

## Import

```ts
// Barrel (recommended — tree-shakes under ESM)
import { Heart, BetweenVerticalStart, AnimateIcon } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'

// Per-icon subpath (guaranteed separate chunks)
import Heart from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue/icons/heart'
```

Alias form — use `<Name>Animated` when you also import the static
`lucide-vue-next`:

```ts
import { Heart as StaticHeart } from 'lucide-vue-next'
import { HeartAnimated } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'
```

## Nuxt

Nuxt 3 apps can register every icon + `<AnimateIcon>` as auto-imports via the
shipped module. No imports needed in templates.

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['@respeak/lucide-motion-vue/nuxt'],
})
```

Default naming is suffixed: `<HeartAnimated>`, `<Link2Animated>`, …, plus
`<AnimateIcon>` unchanged. This is collision-free next to `lucide-vue-next`'s
static `<Heart>`, `<Star>`, etc. Configure with:

```ts
lucideMotion: { prefix: 'M', suffix: '' }   // → <MHeart>, <MLink2>
```

Per-icon tree-shaking is preserved under Nuxt's component loader.

## Triggers (pick one)

Every icon accepts these props. Pick exactly **one** of the triggers, or use
a combination when it makes sense (e.g. hover + tap for interactive icons).

| prop                  | type                | effect                                      |
|-----------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `animateOnHover`      | `boolean \| string` | Play while hovered.                         |
| `animateOnTap`        | `boolean \| string` | Play while pointer is down.                 |
| `animateOnView`       | `boolean \| string` | Play when the icon scrolls into view.       |
| `animate`             | `boolean \| string` | Programmatic. Bind to your state.           |

String form selects a named variant instead of the default.

### Props (also on every icon)

| prop                  | type                                          | default     | description                                                                       |
|-----------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `size`                | `number`                                      | `28`        | Width/height in px.                                                               |
| `strokeWidth`         | `number`                                      | `2`         | SVG stroke width.                                                                 |
| `animation`           | `string`                                      | `'default'` | Which named variant group to use.                                                 |
| `persistOnAnimateEnd` | `boolean`                                     | `false`     | Keep final state after playback.                                                  |
| `initialOnAnimateEnd` | `boolean`                                     | `false`     | Force snap back to initial state.                                                 |
| `clip`                | `boolean`                                     | `false`     | Clip overflow at the wrapper's box. Use for exit animations (send, rocket launch).|
| `triggerTarget`       | `'self' \| 'parent' \| \`closest:${string}\`` | `'self'`    | Bind hover/tap to an ancestor — for drop-in use inside existing button markup.    |

## Patterns

### Basic

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { Heart } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'
</script>

<template>
  <Heart animateOnHover :size="32" />
</template>
```

### Drop into an existing button (`triggerTarget`)

When markup is already `<button><Icon /></button>` and you don't want to
restructure to `as="template"`, bind the trigger to the parent instead:

```vue
<button class="btn">
  <Heart animateOnHover triggerTarget="parent" :size="18" />
  Favorite
</button>
```

For nested wrappers, use `closest:<selector>`:

```vue
<button class="btn">
  <span class="icon-slot">
    <Heart animateOnHover triggerTarget="closest:button" :size="18" />
  </span>
  Favorite
</button>
```

Only `'self'` (the default) and `'parent'` + `closest:…` apply — they bind
listeners to an ancestor DOM element instead of the icon's own span.

### Whole button as trigger (one trigger, anywhere)

Use `<AnimateIcon as="template">` when one trigger should drive **multiple**
icons, or when you want zero extra DOM:

```vue
<AnimateIcon animateOnHover as="template" v-slot="{ on }">
  <button v-on="on" class="btn">
    <Heart :size="18" />
    <Trash2 :size="18" />
    Delete
  </button>
</AnimateIcon>
```

With Vuetify:

```vue
<AnimateIcon animateOnHover as="template" v-slot="{ on }">
  <v-btn color="primary" v-on="on">
    <Heart :size="18" class="mr-2" />
    Favorite
  </v-btn>
</AnimateIcon>
```

Rule of thumb: `triggerTarget` for a single icon inside existing markup;
`as="template"` when many icons share one trigger, or when the wrapper is
a custom component that already spreads `v-on`.

### One trigger, many icons

`<AnimateIcon>` forwards events onto the **first vnode** in its slot — there
is no wrapper element of its own. So if you want one trigger to drive
multiple icons, wrap them in a single element (any element works) so that
event lives on something whose box covers all of them:

```vue
<AnimateIcon animateOnHover>
  <span style="display: inline-flex; gap: 12px">
    <Heart :size="20" />
    <Trash2 :size="20" />
  </span>
</AnimateIcon>
```

Listing siblings directly under `<AnimateIcon>` would only trigger on the
first one (Heart), since only that vnode receives the event handlers.

### Named variants

Some icons ship multiple named animations. Use the `animation` prop:

```vue
<Heart animateOnHover animation="fill" :size="32" />
<Link2 animateOnHover animation="unlink" :size="32" />
<Rocket animateOnView animation="launch" clip :size="32" />
```

List variants at runtime:

```ts
import { iconsMeta } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'

iconsMeta.find(m => m.pascal === 'Heart')?.animations
// → [
//     { name: 'default', source: 'animate-ui' },
//     { name: 'fill',    source: 'animate-ui' },
//   ]

// Names only:
iconsMeta.find(m => m.pascal === 'Heart')?.animations.map(a => a.name)
// → ['default', 'fill']
```

Many icons also carry a `lucide-animated` variant in addition to their
`animate-ui` default — filter by source to discover them:

```ts
iconsMeta
  .filter(m => m.animations.some(a => a.source === 'lucide-animated'))
  .length
// Hundreds of icons, each selectable via animation="lucide-animated"
```

Icons whose upstream pqoqubbw counterpart has a structurally different
shape ship as **numbered siblings** — e.g. `Send2`, `Moon2`, `X2`,
`AlarmClock2` — so both the original and the alternate animation stay
available. Siblings have their own `Pascal` name and default variant.

Forge-generated multi-variant icons can also ship variants whose element
graphs diverge (different path splits, different animated parts) under one
component name. This is wired internally via `<MultiVariantIcon>`; from the
consumer side it's still just `<Bell animation="alt" />` with the same
discoverability through `iconsMeta`.

### Clip overflow for exit animations

A few icons intentionally move parts outside their viewBox — `Send`'s plane
flies off before returning; `Rocket`'s launch variant lifts off. Those
animations read correctly only when overflow is clipped (otherwise the part
visibly tours around the page instead of disappearing). Set `clip`:

```vue
<Send animateOnHover clip />
<Rocket animateOnView clip animation="launch" />
```

Off by default because other icons (e.g. `Link2`'s burst particles) render
outside their box on purpose. Only opt-in for animations that "leave" the
frame.

### Styling

Icons use `currentColor`. Drive color via any CSS `color` value on the icon
or any ancestor. **Do not add a color prop.**

```vue
<Heart animateOnHover class="text-rose-500" />
<Heart animateOnHover style="color: #4f46e5" />
<div style="color: var(--brand)"><Heart animateOnHover /></div>
```

Fill-based variants also tween to `currentColor`, so filled state matches the
surrounding color automatically.

Sizing works via the `size` prop **or** CSS. `class`, `style`, `id`,
`aria-*`, and event handlers all land on the inner `<svg>` — whether the
icon self-wraps (trigger prop set) or not.

```vue
<Heart :size="40" />
<Heart animateOnHover class="w-10 h-10" />
<Heart animateOnHover style="width: 40px; height: 40px" />
```

**`transform` is the one exception.** motion-v writes an inline
`style="transform: …"` on the rendered svg, which beats any class-defined
`transform`. So if you need to apply your own `transform` to a self-wrapped
icon (typically `translateY(-50%)` to center it absolutely inside an
`<input>`), pass it via inline `style=`, not via a class. `mergeProps`
flows the inline style through alongside motion-v's transform.

```vue
<!-- ✅ inline-style transform reaches the svg -->
<div style="position: relative">
  <Search animateOnHover style="position: absolute; left: 10px; top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%); width: 18px; height: 18px" />
  <input style="padding-left: 36px" />
</div>

<!-- ❌ class-based transform is overwritten by motion-v's `transform: none` -->
<style>.input-icon { position: absolute; transform: translateY(-50%); }</style>
<Search animateOnHover class="input-icon" />
```

This only matters for `transform`. Flex/grid centering (e.g. Vuetify
`<v-text-field #prepend-inner>`) needs no CSS at all.

### Programmatic — replay on command

Re-key to remount and replay the mount animation:

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { Bell } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'

const key = ref(0)
function ring() { key.value++ }
</script>

<template>
  <Bell :key="key" animate :size="28" />
  <button @click="ring">Notify</button>
</template>
```

### Programmatic — driven by state

Icons that bake `repeat: Infinity` into their variants
(`LoaderCircle`, `Loader`, `LoaderPinwheel`) loop while mounted with
`animate`:

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'
import { LoaderCircle, Check } from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'

type Status = 'idle' | 'loading' | 'done'
const status = ref<Status>('idle')
</script>

<template>
  <button @click="submit" :disabled="status === 'loading'">
    <LoaderCircle v-if="status === 'loading'" animate :size="16" />
    <Check        v-else-if="status === 'done'"  animate :size="16" />
    Submit
  </button>
</template>
```

### Programmatic — bind a variant

`animate` accepts a variant name. Bind it to state for declarative swaps:

```vue
<Heart :animate="isFavorited ? 'fill' : false" />
```

## Accessibility

- **Decorative** (next to a visible label): `aria-hidden="true"`.
- **Meaningful** (icon-only button): set `aria-label` on the button.
- Animation never changes the DOM or any `aria-*` state, so screen readers
  aren't affected. For `prefers-reduced-motion`, render static icons from
  `lucide-vue-next` instead when the user prefers reduced motion.

## TypeScript

```ts
import type {
  IconTriggerProps,
  IconMeta,
  TriggerTarget,
} from '@respeak/lucide-motion-vue'
```

- Every icon has typed props.
- `IconTriggerProps` is the shared prop set (size/strokeWidth/triggers/clip/triggerTarget).
- `IconMeta` is `{ kebab, pascal, animations: Array<{ name, source }> }`
  where `source` is `'animate-ui' | 'lucide-animated' | 'hand-written'`.
  See `ATTRIBUTIONS.md` for what each source means and its license.
- `TriggerTarget` is `'self' | 'parent' | \`closest:${string}\``.
- No `any` in the public API.

## Do / don't

- **Do** use `triggerTarget="parent"` when dropping a single icon into
  existing `<button><Icon /></button>` markup.
- **Do** wrap with `<AnimateIcon as="template">` when **multiple** icons
  share one trigger, or the hit target is a complex custom component.
- **Do** set `clip` on "exit" animations (`Send`, `Rocket` launch, etc.) so
  the off-viewBox motion reads as "left the frame."
- **Do** use `currentColor` via `color`/Tailwind text utilities.
- **Do** size via the `size` prop *or* CSS (`class="w-6 h-6"`, inline
  `style`) — both reach the inner `<svg>`.
- **Don't** add custom color props — there aren't any.
- **Don't** set `clip` on icons whose variants deliberately paint outside
  the viewBox (`Link2`'s burst particles); it will hide the effect.
- **Don't** render every icon on a page with `animateOnView` (distracting).
  Prefer `animateOnHover` for affordances, reserve `animateOnView` for hero
  moments.
- **Don't** hand-roll `setInterval` to loop icons; use the variants that bake
  `repeat: Infinity` (or re-key to replay one-shots).
